title: Sustainable AI Infrastructure for Practical Teams
description: Join us for a discussion on the environmental cost of AI systems, efficient infrastructure choices, and the tradeoffs teams face when balancing speed, cost, and sustainability.

title: Modern Dev Tools That Actually Improve Team Workflow
description: A conversation for engineers about modern tooling, build pipelines, and the kinds of practical workflow upgrades that genuinely help teams ship faster.

title: What Makes a Feature Worth Building?
description: Explore how thoughtful product decisions, user feedback, and business goals come together when teams decide which features deserve to make the roadmap.

title: Design Trends Worth Paying Attention To in 2026
description: Frontend developers and designers will look at emerging interface patterns, visual trends, and the decisions that help digital products stay useful and current.

title: React State Patterns for Growing Products
description: A hands-on discussion about organizing state, managing complexity, and keeping React applications maintainable as features and team size continue to grow.

title: Small Software Changes That Save Teams Big Time
description: We will look at practical improvements to internal tools, release workflows, and engineering habits that create a surprisingly large productivity payoff over time.

title: Behind the Stack: How Modern ML Platforms Are Built
description: Take a closer look at the infrastructure, experimentation loops, and system design choices behind modern machine learning platforms and applied AI products.

title: Choosing the Right Data Boundaries in Distributed Systems
description: A backend-focused session on service boundaries, persistence decisions, and the tradeoffs that show up when systems become more distributed and interconnected.

title: Building Team Culture That Supports Great Work
description: Join us for a conversation on collaboration, mentorship, and the team habits that help people do strong technical work without burning out.

title: Portfolio Projects That Help Junior Developers Stand Out
description: Early-career developers will share ideas for stronger portfolio projects, clearer case studies, and better ways to present technical growth to hiring teams.

title: From First Issue to Meaningful Contribution
description: A welcoming event for contributors who want to learn how to find approachable issues, communicate with maintainers, and turn first contributions into ongoing involvement.

title: TypeScript Patterns That Keep Codebases Calm
description: We will dig into practical typing strategies, safer abstractions, and the kind of TypeScript patterns that help teams move quickly without losing clarity.

title: Startup Milestones, Setbacks, and Lessons Learned
description: Hear stories about early traction, missed bets, and the decisions that helped a growing company adapt while building toward longer-term goals.

title: Engineering for Sustainability in Real-World Systems
description: Explore how engineers are approaching sustainability through smarter systems, efficient defaults, and practical design decisions that reduce waste without slowing product teams down.

title: Right-Sizing Infrastructure Without Guesswork
description: A practical conversation on observability, cost signals, and how to make better infrastructure sizing decisions before overprovisioning becomes the default.

title: Turning User Research Into Better Interface Decisions
description: A UX-focused event on turning interviews, feedback, and behavioral clues into more intuitive interface decisions and stronger product experiences.

title: Where React Apps Slow Down and What to Fix First
description: This session focuses on bottlenecks in modern React apps, how to spot them early, and what kinds of fixes give the best performance return for the effort.

title: Full Stack Architecture Choices That Age Well
description: Join fellow builders for a conversation on APIs, databases, frontend boundaries, and the architecture decisions that continue to pay off as products evolve.

title: Using Data to Improve Retention and Customer Success
description: Learn how analytics, experimentation, and product feedback loops can improve customer outcomes while helping teams make clearer and faster decisions.

title: Designing APIs That Stay Useful as Products Expand
description: A backend engineering event on API evolution, compatibility, and how to design service contracts that remain workable as product scope keeps growing.

title: Mentorship Habits That Actually Help People Grow
description: We will talk about mentoring in technical teams, giving useful guidance, and building support systems that help newer teammates grow with confidence.

title: Healthy Open Source Collaboration at Scale
description: A session about review culture, contributor onboarding, and the project habits that make open source communities healthier and easier to join.

title: Interview Stories, Resume Signals, and Hiring Patterns
description: Junior developers will compare interview experiences, resume strategies, and the signals that seem to matter most when teams are evaluating early-career candidates.

title: Type-Driven Refactors for Safer Releases
description: A TypeScript-centered discussion on using stronger typing, better tooling, and deliberate refactors to make releases safer as codebases become more complex.
